# localgcp

> The unified GCP emulator. One binary, fourteen services, zero cloud bills.

localgcp is an open source Go binary that emulates Google Cloud Platform services locally for development and testing. It replaces fragmented emulator setups with a single command.

## Services

Nine services are implemented natively in Go (zero dependencies):
- Cloud Storage (REST, port 4443)
- Pub/Sub (gRPC, port 8085)
- Secret Manager (gRPC, port 8086)
- Firestore (gRPC, port 8088)
- Cloud Tasks (gRPC, port 8089)
- Vertex AI (REST, port 8090) — supports Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic backends
- Cloud KMS (gRPC, port 8091)
- Cloud Logging (gRPC, port 8092)
- Cloud Run (gRPC, port 8093)

Five services are orchestrated via Docker containers (opt-in, lazy startup):
- Spanner (gRPC, port 9010) — official Google emulator
- Bigtable (gRPC, port 9094) — official Google emulator
- Cloud SQL (TCP, port 5432) — PostgreSQL 16
- Memorystore (TCP, port 6379) — Redis 7
- BigQuery (REST, port 9060) — DuckDB-powered emulator (LocalBQ)

## Quick Start

```bash
brew install slokam-ai/tap/localgcp
localgcp up
eval $(localgcp env)
```

For orchestrated services (requires Docker):
```bash
localgcp up --services=spanner,bigtable,cloudsql,memorystore
```

## Key Features

- Sub-100ms startup for native services
- Lazy Docker orchestration: containers start only on first connection
- Works with official GCP client libraries (Go, Python, Java, Node.js)
- Vertex AI with local LLM inference via Ollama
- In-memory by default, optional persistence via --data-dir
- CI/CD ready: no credentials needed

## Links

- Website: https://localgcp.com
- Documentation: https://localgcp.com/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/slokam-ai/localgcp
- Install: brew install slokam-ai/tap/localgcp
